name: PCI DSS v4.0.1 — Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard

controls.title: Configuration standards for NSCs are defined, implemented, and maintained
controls.description: Network security controls (formerly firewalls) have documented configuration standards covering ruleset definition, change control, and at-least-annual review.

controls.title: NSCs are implemented between trusted and untrusted networks
controls.description: Network security controls separate the CDE from untrusted networks (Internet, partner networks) and from out-of-scope internal networks.

controls.title: Configuration standards are developed, implemented, and maintained for all system components
controls.description: All in-scope system components have documented hardened configuration baselines aligned to industry standards (CIS, vendor benchmarks).

controls.title: SAD is not retained after authorization, even if encrypted
controls.description: Sensitive Authentication Data (full track data, CAV2/CVC2/CVV2/CID, PIN/PIN block) must not be stored after authorization completion, regardless of encryption.

controls.title: PAN is rendered unreadable anywhere it is stored
controls.description: Primary Account Number stored anywhere must be rendered unreadable using strong cryptography, truncation, tokenization, or one-way hashing with keyed cryptographic hash.

controls.title: Strong cryptography and security protocols are implemented to safeguard PAN during transmission over open, public networks
controls.description: PAN transmitted over open public networks must use strong cryptography (TLS 1.2+, with TLS 1.3 strongly recommended) with proper certificate validation.

controls.title: An anti-malware solution is deployed on all system components, except those identified as not commonly affected
controls.description: Anti-malware solutions are deployed on all in-scope systems; systems excluded must have periodic threat evaluation documented.

controls.title: All system components are protected from known vulnerabilities by installing applicable security patches
controls.description: Critical/high-risk patches must be installed within 1 month of release; other applicable patches within an appropriate timeframe defined by Targeted Risk Analysis.

controls.title: All payment page scripts that are loaded and executed in the consumer's browser are managed
controls.description: Scripts on payment pages must be authorized, integrity-assured (SRI or equivalent), and inventoried — a v4.0 future-dated requirement now mandatory.

controls.title: All user accounts and related access privileges are reviewed at least once every six months
controls.description: Access reviews for user accounts (including third party and application accounts) are performed at least every 6 months with documented approval/revocation.

controls.title: All user access to system components for users and administrators is authenticated using strong authentication factors
controls.description: Authentication uses at least one of: something you know, have, or are. Passwords must meet minimum complexity and rotation requirements.

controls.title: MFA is implemented for all non-console access into the CDE
controls.description: Multi-factor authentication is required for all non-console access to the CDE for all users (including administrators) — mandatory as of 31 March 2025.

controls.title: All media with cardholder data is physically secured
controls.description: All media (paper, electronic) containing CHD is physically secured with controlled access, inventory tracking and secure disposal.

controls.title: Audit logs are enabled and active for all system components and cardholder data
controls.description: Audit logs capture: user access to CHD, root/admin actions, access to audit trails, invalid access attempts, identification/authentication, audit log changes, system object actions.

controls.title: Audit logs are reviewed at least daily for security events and anomalies
controls.description: Audit logs from all in-scope systems are reviewed at least daily, with automated tools acceptable; exceptions are investigated.

controls.title: Internal vulnerability scans are performed at least once every three months
controls.description: Internal vulnerability scans are performed quarterly and after significant changes; all high-risk/critical vulnerabilities are remediated and rescanned.

controls.title: External vulnerability scans are performed by an ASV at least once every three months
controls.description: External-facing systems must be scanned quarterly by a PCI SSC Approved Scanning Vendor (ASV); all findings remediated to achieve passing scan.

controls.title: External and internal penetration testing is performed at least annually and after significant change
controls.description: Penetration testing covers external perimeter and internal critical systems; performed annually and after significant infrastructure changes.

controls.title: A Targeted Risk Analysis is performed for each PCI DSS requirement that the entity meets with the Customized Approach
controls.description: Customized Approach implementations require a documented TRA covering threat landscape, control effectiveness analysis, and monitoring approach — reviewed annually.

controls.title: An incident response plan exists and is ready to be activated in the event of a suspected or confirmed security incident
controls.description: A documented incident response plan covers detection, response, escalation, communication (including card brands), evidence handling, and review/update at least annually.
